All-in-one Workspace

Nautis is the AI-native Operating System for founders. Manage fundraising, planning, finance, documents, meetings, CRM, hiring, and daily operations from one connected workspace. Unlike disconnected SaaS tools, every module shares one AI brain, giving you context-aware automation, smarter insights, and a Chief of Staff that understands your business—not just one app. Spend less time managing tools and more time building.

results.title: 【F】 Top 5 Alternatives To The Patek Philippe Nautilus 5711 (2022)
results.content:
Reply
19. Don’t you have watches priced  
     200 to 400 € ?

    Reply

    1. Not as Nautilus alternative.

       Reply

       1. Double down and take the Straum.  
           Live a little ?

          But don’t let my bad humor demoralize you, there’s always the infamous seikonaut ( I think that’s what they call it… ?)

          Anyways, a Seiko 5 with very similar broad ears. Very inflated now for a 75€ watch, but still in your price range.

          Reply

          1. It wasn’t my question 😉 I personally don’t believe in €200/€400 Nautilus alternatives ;-), for that money I’d get a Casioak and be happy.

             Reply

             1. We can’t fault a man for the ears appeal ? [...] Reply

    1. Hey ROb, thanks for your comment. That is quite a collection! I don’t think that an alternative has to compare and stand up in the exact same way quality-wise etc, but if the watch can carry some of the spirit and essence of what makes the original so iconic then that’s a job well done in my eyes.

       I really enjoyed the Czapek. It’s a little beyond my budget (read as “very”) but if I had the money I think I’d strongly consider it. The SXH5 movement is one of the nicest-looking calibers out there in my opinion.

       I’m keen to one day pick up one a Laventure for my collection. I fear I may have missed out on nabbing one at a good price though. They’r eonly going to go up from here I think! [...] + Art of Horology

results.title: Six More Affordable Alternatives to the Patek Philippe Nautilus | Gear Patrol
results.content:
Read more
 ### Formex Essence ThirtyNine

   Read more
 ### Frederique Constant Highlife Automatic COSC

   Read more
 ### IWC Ingenieur

   Read more
 ### Chopard Alpine Eagle

   Read more

### Yema Urban Traveler

green yema watchYema

### Yema Urban Traveller

Yema’s take on the integrated bracelet trend features a winning combination of a ’70s-inspired design, a honeycomb textured dial and an in-house automatic movement — all in a package that costs under $900.

#### Specs

|  |  |
 --- |
| Case Size | 39mm |
| Movement | Yema Cal. YEMA2000 automatic |
| Water Resistance | 100m |

#### Pros

 Great value
 Excellent sizing

#### Cons

 Dial looks cheap compared to competitors
 In-house movement's longterm performance is untested

 $890 at Yema [...] The finishing is solid and it’s powered by the same movement as the men’s 41mm version (which does not come with a ~~Tiffany~~ light blue dial), but I’d love to see the ladies version get the same COSC certification and 100m water resistance as the men’s model. Women (and men with small wrists) also appreciate accuracy and swim with their watches.

### IWC Ingenieur

watchIWC

### IWC Ingenieur

#### Specs

|  |  |
 --- |
| Case Size | 40mm |
| Movement | IWC Cal. 32111 (Val Fleurier base) |
| Water Resistance | 100m |

#### Pros

 A true Genta design, modernized
 Absolutely gorgeous dial

#### Cons

 The price seems overly ambitious
 The movement should be better for the money

 $11,700.00 at iwc.com [...] ### Chopard Alpine Eagle

Chopard

### Chopard Alpine Eagle

Looking something like a cross between a Patek Nautilus and an AP Royal Oak while still managing to stand out on its own, the Alpine Eagle has quickly become one of the best integrated bracelet models on the market. What makes it so great? Take your pick between the sweet in-house movement, the stunning swirled dial modeled after an eagle’s iris or the minuscule thickness of just 9.7mm.

#### Specs

|  |  |
 --- |
| Case Size | 41mm |
| Movement | Chopard Cal. 01.01-C automatic |
| Water Resistance | 100m |

#### Pros

 A striking dial unlike anything else
 Fantastic in-house movement

#### Cons

 The design is polarizing
 A bit large at 41mm; 39mm would be money

 $14,400 at Neiman Marcus

results.title: NAUTIS Home: Maritime & Ship Simulator for Everyone!
results.content:
Simulators
  + Image
  + Pages
    - NAUTIS SimulatorWith NAUTIS, we enable learning by simulation through assisting organizations in (re-)training their maritime students for real-life adversities in safe but true-to-life conditions.
    - Portable SimulatorThe Portable Simulator comes with Radar, ECDIS, Outside View, Conning Panel and Azimuth controls or Conventional controls.
    - NAUTIS VisualiserThe NAUTIS Visualiser enables you to scale from merely visuals up to a full, professional simulations including all imaginable systems and calculations.
    - NAUTIS HomeThe maritime simulator for everyone! NAUTIS Home enables both maritime professionals and enthusiasts to experience realistic ship simulation.
 Simulators
  + NAUTIS Home
  + Portable Simulator
  + NAUTIS Visualiser [...] Realistic 3D simulation

#### Realistic 3D simulation

NAUTIS Home is based on our professional NAUTIS software. This results in an immersive, 3D simulation experience with realistic hydrodynamics and vessel behavior. You can train your skills and sail in highly realistic settings.

LEARN MORE

Nautical training

#### Nautical training

Through scenarios you can experience different aspects of nautical training. Learn to make a sharp turn, or train to manoeuvre in high traffic waters and narrow straits. Work your way up from beginner-level manoeuvring to more advanced.

Briefing and assessment

#### Briefing and assessment [...] Realistic 3D simulation

#### Realistic 3D simulation

NAUTIS Home is based on our professional NAUTIS software. This results in an immersive, 3D simulation experience with realistic hydrodynamics and vessel behavior. You can train your skills and sail in highly realistic settings.

Nautical training

#### Nautical training

Through scenarios you can experience different aspects of nautical training. Learn to make a sharp turn, or train to manoeuvre in high traffic waters and narrow straits. Work your way up from beginner-level manoeuvring to more advanced.

Briefing and assessment

#### Briefing and assessment
